Strategy Description: A strategy that buys stocks that have performed well in the past and sells those that have performed poorly, expecting the trend to continue.
Core Logic: Stocks that outperformed peers over a 3-12 month period tend to continue to perform well in the upcoming period.
Entry Conditions: Go long stocks with high relative past 12-month returns (skipping the most recent month) and short those with low returns.
Risk Management Rules: Managing time-varying specific risk can eliminate crashes and double the Sharpe ratio.
Market Regime Suitability: Prone to 'momentum crashes' during market rebounds following large declines.
Volatility Considerations: Estimated volatility of 16.6%; very high drawdown potential (-87.41%).
Full Notes: Based on Jegadeesh and Titman (1993). Behavioral explanations include herding and over/underreaction.
